violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Employee has registered in an approved food safety management course. Once successfully completed employee will register with RIDOH Center for Food Protection as the Certified Food Safety Manager. New Violation. Correct By: 05/07/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Establishment does have employees certified through an approved food safety management course. One of these employees must register with RIDOH Center for Food Protection as the Certified Food Safety Manager. Application provided. In addition, there was no employee certified through an approved food safety management course available at the time of inspection. There must be an employee present during all hours of operation certified through an approved food safety management course. List provided. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (deli slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/27/2026

Ziti, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Ziti observed in the sandwich prep unit and the walk-in cooler with a temperature of 50 - 52 degrees F. Voluntary disposal during inspection. Proper cooling reviewed. New Violation. Correct By: 04/27/2026

Foods (pasta, deli meats) observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 04/27/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Ziti was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. Proper cooling methods reviewed. New Violation. Correct By: 04/27/2026

Spinach was observed thawing on the drying rack. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/27/2026
Onions, lettuce, and tomatoes were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/27/2026
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3-bay sink. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2026
The non-food contact surfaces of the walk-in condenser fan guard have an accumulation of black residue and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 04/27/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. An employee certified in food safety management must register with RIDOH Center for Food Protection as the Certified Food Safety Manager. Application provided. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2025

Thermometers are lacking in the prep units. Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 05/30/2025
Refrigerator door gaskets of the sandwich prep unit observed in poor repair. Gaskets must be replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 05/31/2025
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3-bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 05/06/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the walk-in condenser fan guard have an accumulation of black residue and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2025

The cutting boards are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2024
The floor in the walk-in cooler is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2024
